name: Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop
name_original: Puerto de Palombera - Fontibre – circular desde Reinosa por la Transcantábrica
description: Experience the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop, a moderate 22.1-mile mountain biking route with stunning views and challenging terrain
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campoo De Enmedio features a diverse landscape characterized by mountain passes, river valleys, and varied elevations. The region offers routes that traverse both open terrain and areas near historical towns, providing a mix of natural and cultural points of interest. Elevation gains are common, with trails often leading through hilly and mountainous areas. Best mountain bike trails around Campoo De Enmedio The most popular mountain bike trail is Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop, a 22.1 miles (35.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Palombera Pass. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Palombera Pass – Cruz de Fuentes loop from Reinosa, a difficult 41.3 miles (66.5 km) path. This route includes significant elevation changes and expansive mountain scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Cuenca Bucer – Iglesia de Espinilla loop from Reinosa, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Cuenca Bucer basin, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Campoo De Enmedio is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and diverse elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Campoo De Enmedio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campoo De Enmedio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails in Campoo De Enmedio, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What are the general characteristics of mountain biking in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Mountain biking in Campoo De Enmedio is characterized by a diverse landscape featuring mountain passes, river valleys, and varied elevations. Trails often lead through hilly and mountainous areas, offering a mix of natural beauty and cultural points of interest near historical towns.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: While the majority of trails are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. Most routes involve significant elevation changes, so even moderate trails require a good level of fitness.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: You can expect varied terrain, from open areas to routes near historical towns. Many trails feature significant elevation changes, traversing mountain passes and river valleys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuenca Bucer – Iglesia de Espinilla loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Cuenca Bucer basin.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass – Cruz de Fuentes loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 41.3-mile (66.5 km) path with significant elevation changes, offering expansive mountain scenery.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Campoo De Enmedio are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000368765/transcantabrica-palombera-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5342054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: The trails offer a mix of natural and historical sights. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, or pass through a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;beautiful section through the beech forest&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable points include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3277001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options?
A: While the region is known for its moderate to difficult trails, the single easy trail available would be the most suitable starting point for families or those new to mountain biking. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about trails in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: The trails in Campoo De Enmedio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging elevation changes, and the scenic beauty of mountain passes and river valleys.

Q: Which mountain bike trails offer views of the Palombera Pass?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000368765/transcantabrica-palombera-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that specifically offers views of the Palombera Pass. Another option is the more difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass – Cruz de Fuentes loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, which also includes expansive mountain scenery around the pass.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that pass through historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes integrate historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5342054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; allows you to explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt; near some trails.
tips:
On a hill, and guarding the road that linked the coast with Castilla through the Saja Valley, is the Castillo de San Vicente, in Argüeso, within the municipality of the Brotherhood of Campoo de Suso, in the South from Cantabria (Spain).
Built during the 13th, 14th and 15th centuries, it was the emblem and fortress of the dominion of the House of Mendoza in the highlands of Campoo.
The Palombera pass is a 1st category mountain pass located at the eastern end of the Sierra del Cordel. One of the most beautiful ports in Cantabria with a spectacular landscape.
The town of Fontibre is recognized because right here is the spring that indicates the birthplace of the Ebro River, one of the most important in Spain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking loop offers a challenging experience with rugged terrain. You can expect demanding ascents and technical descents, often involving significant elevation changes. Some sections may feature steep paths, stony tracks, and even require you to push or carry your bike due to the extreme nature of the challenge. The route traverses mountains, passes, gorges, and forests.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop is approximately 35.6 km long with nearly 900 meters of elevation gain. On average, it takes about 3 hours and 22 minutes to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level and technical skill.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers spectacular scenery and several notable points. You'll pass by the historic Castillo de Argüeso, experience the impressive views from Palombera Pass, and ride near the source of the Ebro River at Fontibre. The Palombera Pass itself is known for its panoramic views of the Cantabrian Mountains.

faqs.question: Is the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is rated as moderate but the terrain in the Cantabrian Mountains is generally considered challenging to extreme for mountain biking. It requires significant physical fitness and advanced technical skills due to demanding ascents, technical descents, and rugged paths. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: The Cantabrian Mountains can experience varied weather. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, generally, late spring through early autumn (May to October) offers the most favorable conditions for mountain biking in mountainous regions, avoiding snow and the harshest winter weather. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific loop is not explicitly stated. However, when mountain biking in natural areas, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain. Always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that specific permits or entrance fees are required to ride the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop. However, it's always a good practice to check for any local regulations or protected area rules before your visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop?
faqs.answer: The tour description indicates the loop starts and ends near Reinosa. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, Reinosa would be the logical starting point for parking and accessing the loop. Look for public parking areas within or on the outskirts of the town.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop incorporates sections of several other official trails. Notably, it follows parts of the Transcantábrica (43% of the route), the Camino natural de Ebro - Cantabria (13%), and the Sendero Histórico: Cantabria (11%). You'll also find smaller segments of [CIMA CA15] Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo and [CIMA CA04] Palombera.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near Reinosa, a town that would offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Along the route itself, especially in more remote mountain sections, facilities will be scarce. It's best to plan for provisions in Reinosa before you start.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Cantabrian Mountains are rich in biodiversity. While specific wildlife sightings are not guaranteed, the region is home to various species. Keep an eye out for local birds, and potentially larger mammals, though they are often shy. Always observe wildlife from a respectful distance.
